trigger: Reject any telephony vendor that lacks callback authenticity proof or forces blind trust in unsigned voice events.
Reject any telephony vendor if call accepted or callback arrival would be mistaken for settled contact truth.
Reject any SMS vendor that cannot surface queued, delayed, bounced, expired, or wrong-recipient states separately.
Reject any SMS vendor whose callback channel cannot be authenticated or made replay-safe with documented provider signals.
Reject any email vendor that lacks signed or replay-safe webhook proof for delivery, bounce, and complaint events.
Reject any email vendor that cannot preserve sender/domain verification, event webhook setup, and environment separation cleanly.
Reject any combined-suite option that wins only by procurement simplicity while increasing lock-in and failure-domain coupling.
effect: Not shortlistable as a cross-family default.

trigger: Reject any combined-suite option that does not cover telephony, SMS, and email together in current reviewed official docs.
Reject any internal twin that skips IVR branches, urgent preemption, webhook disorder, bounce/dispute, or route-repair semantics.
effect: Mock lane not selectable for MVP execution.

trigger: Explicit seq_031 rejection: Postmark current official guidance exposes custom headers/basic auth but no signed replay-safe callback proof.
Reject any combined-suite proposal if seq_032 or seq_033 would be unable to swap vendors per family without lifecycle rewrites.
effect: No combined suite may be defaulted now.
